userIntent: Create a zero-downtime Postgres migration plan.

state.title: Zero-downtime Postgres migration plan
state.summary: Migrate customer traffic with dual writes, backfill verification, read shadowing, and a reversible cutover. The safest path is expand → backfill → dual-read verification → cutover → contract, with every phase gated by checks.
goals: Move reads and writes to the new schema without planned downtime
Preserve rollback until after post-cutover validation
Detect replication or backfill drift before customers are affected
Keep application deploys decoupled from destructive database changes
nonGoals: Rewriting the entire data access layer
Optimizing historical query performance beyond migration safety

assumptions.text: Application can write both old and new columns during the migration window.
Backfill jobs can be throttled below production saturation.

decisions.rationale: It separates additive changes from destructive cleanup.

decisions.choice: Row counts, checksums, and shadow reads
decisions.rationale: Catches correctness drift before traffic moves.

architecture.caption: The application keeps both schemas valid until validation proves the new read path is safe.

phases.name: Expand schema
phases.gate: New nullable columns and indexes deployed with no lock-risk operations.
tasks: Deploy code that can read old path only
validation: Migration applies in staging with production-like data

phases.name: Dual write
phases.gate: Every new write populates both old and new schema.
validation: Mismatch rate remains zero for new writes

phases.name: Backfill and verify
phases.gate: Historical rows are copied and checksums match.
validation: Checksum mismatch is zero or explained

phases.name: Shadow reads and cutover
phases.gate: Shadow reads match and read flag can be enabled gradually.

phases.name: Contract old schema
phases.gate: Rollback window closed and no consumers read old fields.
validation: No old field reads in logs

risks.risk: Long-running index or migration blocks writes
risks.signal: Lock wait alerts during expand phase
risks.mitigation: Use concurrent index creation and split DDL into small reviewed changes

risks.signal: CPU, IOPS, or replication lag rises above threshold
risks.mitigation: Throttle job and pause automatically on lag

risks.risk: Application rollback writes only old schema after new reads start
risks.mitigation: Keep dual-write code backward compatible until contract phase

risks.risk: Unknown downstream consumer depends on old columns
risks.signal: Query logs show old column reads
risks.mitigation: Audit query logs before cleanup and delay destructive drops

validation: psql migration dry run on staging snapshot
